title: Canonical-content SHA-256 dedup for decision fixtures - the final structural defense

evidence.ref: PR #97 landed with id 000-fixture-positive-governance-topdown and title 'Positive fixture: top-down accepted governance decision' — no dumb-agent substring, clean filename, clean purpose. The predicted exhaustion of D028's 6-layer structural fingerprinting. Fixture content is structurally identical to canonical decision-valid-minimal modulo identity-only fields
028-dumb-agent-title-marker — D028's 6 structural layers (which D030 supersedes as the final defense)
015-fixture-purpose-required-and-dedup — D015's purpose-string dedup is the first dedup defense; D030 is the canonical-content-shape dedup that catches what wording variations slip past
fixtures/positive/decision/decision-positive-governance-topdown.* — pre-deletion sample of the D028-bypass that D030 now catches

guards.ref: internal/probe.VerifyFixtures — seenContentHashes map keyed by (category, fixture_type, sha256(canonicalDecisionContentHash(data))); the canonicalizer strips id/title/examples/counterexamples/evidence/created_at/notes and re-marshals so map-key ordering is deterministic
concept-map C-015 — codifies the rule and the stripped-field set

examples: Bypass fixture from PR #97 vs canonical decision-valid-minimal: stripping (id, title, examples, counterexamples, evidence, created_at) leaves {affected_repos, generated_artifacts:[], guards:[], owner, scope, source, ssot_owner, status} identical → same canonical hash → D030 rejects the second occurrence.
A future fixture with status=proposed instead of accepted has a different canonical hash → D030 accepts it because it exercises a new validator path.
A future fixture with scope.areas=['observability'] instead of ['governance'] has a different canonical hash → accepted because the scope content differs.
counterexamples: ir-aggregate, ir-event, query fixtures are intentionally NOT covered by D030; they distinguish on `name` which encodes the entity being modeled, so a name-aware canonicalizer is required. Follow-up decision when warranted.

Identity fields (id, title, examples, counterexamples, evidence, created_at, notes) being different is NOT enough to pass — those don't drive validator outcomes. Two fixtures must differ in schema-relevant content (status, scope, source, etc.) to both be accepted.
